stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 3 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 8 observations with mean 84.4 % (within-trial site SD = 8.8). trial site B has 5 observations with mean 69.7 % (within-trial site SD = 7.8). trial site C has 15 observations with mean 80.7 % (within-trial site SD = 6.8). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.2 and 12.9 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site A: 83.9 % (shrunk from raw 84.4). Range [83.2, 84.1] as between-trial site SD varies from 5.2 to 12.9. Pooled mean: 79.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 79.8 % (weighted average of 3 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/8). At σ_between=8.6: B = 0.884.
Interval [83.2, 84.1] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (84.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (79.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
